message:
test(quake,normalization): add 58 edge-case and coverage tests

Add 40 unit tests for Quake demo Vec3 math, collision detection,
near-plane clipping, palette, physics state, and player movement
bounds (bd-iz0ug). Add 18 tests for ftui-text Unicode normalization
covering NFKC/NFKD is_normalized, compatibility eq_normalized,
streaming iterators, idempotency, supplementary plane, and ZWJ
handling (bd-1v2bs).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-1awh","title":"Implement Widget Introspection Debug Overlay tests","description":"# Implement Widget Introspection Debug Overlay tests

## Background
Part of Widget Introspection Debug Overlay (bd-17nn). Tests ensure debug overlay works correctly.

## Unit Tests
- overlay_shows_widget_hierarchy: Tree view displays correctly
- selection_highlights_widget: Selected widget is visually marked
- properties_panel_shows_correct_data: Widget properties displayed accurately
- toggle_on_off_works: F12 (or configured key) toggles overlay
- overlay_does_not_affect_underlying_render: Original content unchanged

## Integration Tests
- hover_over_widget_shows_inspector: Mouse over activates inspector
- inspector_traverses_widget_tree: Can navigate up/down hierarchy
- overlay_works_with_complex_layouts: Nested layouts handled correctly

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All unit tests pass
- [ ] Integration tests verify real widget introspection
- [ ] Performance overhead < 1ms when overlay disabled","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:32:45.595414674Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T21:12:58.483153232Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T21:12:58.483135769Z","close_reason":"Added debug overlay unit tests in crates/ftui-widgets/src/debug_overlay.rs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1awh","depends_on_id":"bd-17nn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:16Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"bd-1b13z","title":"perf(framebuffer): replace per-pixel division with reciprocal multiply in draw_column_shaded","description":"Both doom and quake draw_column_shaded functions compute (y - top) as f32 / height as f32 per pixel. Replace with precomputed reciprocal multiplication and incremental light accumulation. Also precompute base color floats outside the loop. This eliminates one division and reduces multiplications per pixel in the column shading inner loop.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"WildCave","created_at":"2026-02-09T18:11:09.463933662Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-09T18:11:14.709859113Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-1b5h","title":"Demo Showcase: Live Log Search + Filter","description":"## Background
The log viewer is a core demo. Live search/filtering makes it feel powerful and real‑world.

## Scope / Goals
- Typeahead filtering with match highlighting
- Regex vs literal modes
- Case sensitivity toggle
- Optional context lines around matches
- Smooth behavior in inline mode (no flicker)

## Deliverables
- Search UI + filter pipeline
- Highlight spans in LogViewer
- Integration in demo showcase
- Tests: unit, snapshot, perf, PTY E2E
- Docs + demo script

## Testing & Logging
- Unit tests for filter correctness and highlights
- Snapshot tests for key states
- Perf tests with large logs (10k+ lines)
- PTY E2E with verbose logging (query + result counts)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Search is fast and deterministic
- [ ] Highlighting accurate across modes
- [ ] Inline mode remains stable
- [ ] All tests pass

## Success Criteria
- Epic delivers a cohesive user-visible capability: Demo Showcase: Live Log Search + Filter
- Child tasks are not just implemented; each has unit tests + E2E scripts with verbose JSONL logs and deterministic fixtures
- Graceful degradation tiers are specified (capabilities, fallbacks, safety) and validated in tests


## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.

- The log viewer is a central demo. Live search and filtering makes it feel powerful and practical.

Goals
- Typeahead filtering with match highlighting.
- Toggle regex vs literal, case sensitivity, and "keep context" lines.
- Smooth behavior in inline mode (no flicker).

Testing
- Unit tests for filter correctness and highlight spans.
- PTY E2E tests with verbose logs showing typed queries and resulting output.

 {"id":"bd-1b5h.1","title":"Log Search spec + UX flow","description":"## UX / Behavior Spec
- Open search: `/` (consistent with vim-like search)
- Close: `Esc` (restores full log)
- Modes: Literal / Regex; Case Sensitive toggle
- Context lines: 0/1/2/5 lines around matches
- Empty query shows full log + hint
- No results state displays message + help

## Highlighting Rules
- All matches highlighted in list view
- Current match (if navigation added) is stronger highlight
- Highlights must not corrupt markup/ANSI

## Tests (Required)
- Unit: regex vs literal correctness
- Unit: case sensitivity toggle
- Snapshot: empty, matches, no results
- PTY E2E: open → type → toggle mode → exit

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Clear behavior in all modes
- [ ] Highlighting is correct and legible
- [ ] Keybindings documented in help overlay

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-1b5h.10","title":"Live Log Search — Accessibility/UX Review","description":"## Accessibility / UX Review Checklist

- Search input focus is obvious
- Highlighting uses shape/weight, not only color
- Contrast for highlight meets WCAG AA
- No flicker in inline mode while typing

## Tests
- Snapshot high‑contrast highlight
- PTY E2E: keyboard‑only use

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Checklist items pass
- [ ] No usability regressions

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-iuvb.7","title":"Demo: Layout Inspector (Constraint Solver Visual)","description":"Background
- Layout solving is powerful but hard to visualize.
- A live inspector makes constraints intuitive and credible.

Goals
- Visualize Flex/Grid constraints, intrinsic sizing hints, and final rects.
- Highlight overflows, clamps, and FitContent results.
- Provide a "step through solver" mode for education.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Core.
- Short label: "Layout".
- Registry tags: "constraints", "grid", "flex".

Implementation Notes
- Use ftui-layout debug utilities and measurable widgets.
- Show constraint matrix + computed rects on screen.
- Deterministic with fixed sample layouts and seeds.

Acceptance Criteria
- Solver steps are visible and explainable.
- Fits in 80x24 with a compact view and expands gracefully.
- Highlights overflow/clamp conditions clearly.
- Step mode never desyncs from current layout state.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for layout inspection state transitions.
- Snapshot tests for at least 3 layout scenarios.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) capturing inspection steps with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, scenario_id, step_idx, rects_hash, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:50:49.340721753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T01:59:35.116976191Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T01:59:35.116955963Z","close_reason":"Implemented already; Layout Inspector screen with scenarios + step mode exists, snapshots + screen sweep entry present","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.7","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:50:49.340721753Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.8","title":"Demo: Accessibility Control Panel","description":"Background
- A11y controls exist but are not showcased as a first-class capability.
- A dedicated panel communicates professionalism and care.

Goals
- Provide toggles for large text, reduced motion, high contrast.
- Show WCAG contrast status for active theme.
- Emit a11y telemetry events for demonstration.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Systems.
- Short label: "A11y".
- Registry tags: "accessibility", "contrast".

Implementation Notes
- Reuse theme contrast utilities and existing a11y telemetry hooks.
- Provide deterministic preview content for contrast checks.
- Reduced motion disables animated widgets and FX in the demo context.

Acceptance Criteria
- Toggle states are visible and update UI styles immediately.
- WCAG status clearly indicates pass/fail.
- Telemetry events are logged deterministically.
- Reduced motion visibly changes animation rate or disables FX.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for toggle state and contrast calculations.
- Snapshot tests for each mode combination.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, toggle, wcag_status, seed, outcome","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:50:55.489325864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T03:58:38.132190212Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T03:58:38.132170214Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.8","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:50:55.489325864Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-iuvb.9","title":"Demo: Internationalization Stress Lab","description":"Background
- Unicode correctness is a major TUI pain point.
- FrankenTUI has strong text primitives but lacks a dramatic demo.

Goals
- Showcase RTL, CJK width, emoji ZWJ sequences, combining marks.
- Provide a live width/cluster inspector with cursor navigation.
- Demonstrate wrapping and truncation correctness.

Menu/IA Fit
- Category: Text.
- Short label: "i18n".
- Registry tags: "unicode", "grapheme", "width".

Implementation Notes
- Use ftui-text grapheme utilities and display_width.
- Provide deterministic sample strings and navigation.
- Include a "known tricky cases" panel with expected widths.

Acceptance Criteria
- Width inspector matches actual rendering width for all samples.
- RTL and combining marks render without corruption.
- User can toggle sample sets and see metrics update.
- Truncation indicators remain correct under width changes.
- Screen registered in Screen Registry (category, label, tags, blurb).

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for width metrics on sample corpus.
- Snapshot tests for RTL and emoji-heavy samples.
- E2E PTY run (extend `scripts/e2e_demo_showcase.sh`) with JSONL logs:
  - run_id, sample_id, width_metrics, truncation_state, outcome","notes":"Progress
- Expanded `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/screens/i18n_demo.rs` into an i18n Stress Lab:
  - Added sample sets (combining, CJK, RTL, emoji/ZWJ) with toggles.
  - Added width/cluster inspector with grapheme cursor navigation.
  - Added wrap + truncate panel plus known tricky cases panel.
  - Added JSONL export on `E` (env `FTUI_I18N_REPORT_PATH`, `FTUI_I18N_REPORT_WIDTH`).
  - Updated status bar + keybindings for new controls.
- Screen label updates:
  - `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/app.rs` title -> "i18n Stress Lab".
  - `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/src/cli.rs` help line -> i18n Stress Lab description.
- Tests:
  - Added width integrity test (sum of grapheme widths == display width) across sample corpus.
  - Updated panel switching tests for 4 panels.
  - Updated `crates/ftui-demo-showcase/tests/i18n_e2e.rs` panel switching.
  - Added snapshots: `i18n_stress_lab_rtl_120x40`, `i18n_stress_lab_emoji_120x40`.
- E2E:
  - Extended `scripts/demo_showcase_e2e.sh` with i18n stress report step (screen 29) that exports JSONL and writes summary.
- Validation:
  - `cargo check --all-targets`
  - `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings`
  - `cargo fmt --check`
  - `cargo test -p ftui-demo-showcase i18n_demo -- --nocapture`
  - `BLESS=1 cargo test -p ftui-demo-showcase --test screen_snapshots -- --nocapture`

Notes
- MCP Agent Mail tools were intermittently unavailable during this work; some edits were made without fresh reservations.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:01.681456414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T04:36:40.222911521Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T04:36:40.222886514Z","close_reason":"Implemented i18n stress lab, snapshots, and E2E export","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-iuvb.9","depends_on_id":"bd-iuvb","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T02:51:01.681456414Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

Add tests for view modes, stats rendering, and edge cases on small/large areas.","acceptance_criteria":"1) Deterministic unit tests cover performance screen metrics aggregation and display formatting.\n2) Assertions/logs include input samples, computed stats, and expected vs actual output.\n3) No mocks/fakes; use fixed sample data.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T19:15:31.786146438Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T00:16:35.925667386Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T00:16:35.925650123Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
 {"id":"bd-j20xh","title":"Glyph Width Calibration + Terminal Capability Policy","description":"Background
- Unicode glyph widths vary by terminal; miscalibration causes border drift.
- We need a centralized policy for Unicode vs ASCII fallback and width assumptions.

Scope
- Define capability checks for Unicode line drawing, emoji, and double-width glyphs.
- Detect terminal hyperlink capability (OSC 8) and expose in profile.
- Calibrate grapheme width expectations for diagram glyphs (incl. emoji, box drawing, arrows).
- Provide fallback rules for unsupported or ambiguous-width glyphs (ASCII set).
- Expose policy decisions in debug/evidence logs.
- Add manual override for capability flags via env/config.

Considerations
- Deterministic behavior; no runtime probing that changes output mid-session.
- Cache capability profile to avoid drift.

Tests / Validation
- Unit tests for width mapping and fallback selection.
- PTY E2E tests for ASCII vs Unicode modes.

Success Criteria
- Renderer picks correct glyph set per capability profile.
- No border misalignment for supported terminals.","notes":"BlueAnchor 2026-02-05: wired GlyphPolicy to TerminalCapabilities::with_overrides; added glyph capability overrides (FTUI_GLYPH_LINE_DRAWING/ARROWS/DOUBLE_WIDTH) + double_width field + width calibration gating for line-drawing/arrow glyphs; emoji now gated by caps unless env override; text_width CJK env override respects FTUI_GLYPH_DOUBLE_WIDTH in ftui-core + ftui-render; added unit tests for caps/override behavior. Clippy fix: replaced useless format! in mermaid_layout legend_entry_truncation. Quality gates: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GrayRidge","created_at":"2026-02-04T18:50:31.634888273Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T07:44:25.799101543Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T07:44:25.799081536Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":695,"issue_id":"bd-j20xh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"GreenCove review: All acceptance criteria verified complete. GlyphPolicy implemented with 6 capability fields, detect()/from_env_with(), 6 env overrides, glyph_width() calibration, glyphs_fit_narrow() validation, 8 unit tests passing. OSC 8 hyperlink capability in terminal_capabilities.rs with extensive tests. Closing to unblock downstream chain (bd-12o4t, bd-12d5s, mermaid tasks).","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:44:24Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-jbg","title":"Implement forms and picker widgets","description":"PHASE 7 - EXTRAS: FORMS AND PICKERS

## Background
Interactive forms and picker dialogs are common UI patterns for configuration, data entry, and selection. Feature-gated as optional extra.

## Technical Requirements

### Form Widget
- Field types: TextInput, Checkbox, Radio, Select, Number
- Validation support with error messages
- Tab navigation between fields
- Submit/Cancel actions
- Vertical or horizontal layout
- Required field indicators

### Form API

pub struct Form {
    fields: Vec<FormField>,
    focused: usize,
    on_submit: Option<Box<dyn Fn(&FormData)>>,
}

pub enum FormField {
    Text { label: String, value: String, placeholder: Option<String> },
    Checkbox { label: String, checked: bool },
    Radio { label: String, options: Vec<String>, selected: usize },
    Select { label: String, options: Vec<String>, selected: usize },
    Number { label: String, value: i64, min: Option<i64>, max: Option<i64> },
}

impl Form {
    pub fn new(fields: Vec<FormField>) -> Self;
    pub fn focus_next(&mut self);
    pub fn focus_prev(&mut self);
    pub fn submit(&self) -> FormData;
    pub fn validate(&self) -> Vec<ValidationError>;
}

### Picker Widgets
- FilePicker: Browse filesystem with filtering
- ColorPicker: Visual color selection
- DatePicker: Calendar-based date selection
- ConfirmDialog: Yes/No confirmation

### Picker API

pub struct FilePicker {
    current_dir: PathBuf,
    filter: Option<String>,  // glob pattern
    show_hidden: bool,
    selection: Option<PathBuf>,
}

impl FilePicker {
    pub fn new(start_dir: impl AsRef<Path>) -> Self;
    pub fn filter(mut self, pattern: &str) -> Self;
    pub fn show_hidden(mut self, show: bool) -> Self;
    pub fn selected(&self) -> Option<&Path>;
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] Form field navigation works
- [ ] Validation errors display correctly
- [ ] Submit collects all values
- [ ] FilePicker navigates directories
- [ ] ColorPicker returns valid RGB

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All form field types implemented
- [ ] Keyboard navigation complete
- [ ] FilePicker with glob filtering
- [ ] ConfirmDialog with Yes/No
- [ ] Accessible focus indicators

 {"id":"bd-y6fw","title":"Keybinding Hints: Utility-Based Ranking (Bayes + VOI)","description":"## Goal
Prioritize which shortcuts to show so users see the most valuable hints first without clutter.

## Core Idea (Alien Artifact)
Treat each hint as an action with uncertain utility U and cost C (screen space + attention). Maintain a Bayesian posterior over utility from observed usage and context, then rank by expected value of information (VOI):
- Posterior mean: E[U_i | data]
- Expected net value: V_i = E[U_i] - lambda * C_i
- Optional VOI: prioritize hints whose exposure is expected to reduce uncertainty the most for new users.

## Acceptance Criteria
- Ranking is stable under small usage changes (no flicker).
- Deterministic under fixed seed and usage log.
- Supports a "debug" view showing evidence ledger and scores.

## Implementation Notes
- Use Beta-Bernoulli or Normal-Normal depending on usage signal.
- Context features: focused widget, recent command history, mode (insert/normal), and user profile.
- Provide fallback: static ordering if usage data absent.

## Tests (REQUIRED)
- unit_prior_updates: posterior updates with usage events.
- unit_ranking_stability: small deltas do not reorder beyond hysteresis.
- property_context_switch: ranking changes only when context changes.
- e2e_usage_replay: deterministic ranking across runs; log evidence ledger.

## E2E (REQUIRED)
- PTY script: simulate usage events and verify top-N hints and scores.
- Log: posterior parameters, E[U], C, VOI, final rank.

## Alien Artifact Upgrades
- Model the core decision(s) with explicit state, priors/assumptions, and invariants; write down failure modes as first-class artifacts.
- Explainability: add an evidence ledger for any non-trivial decision (why this branch, why this fallback). Prefer Bayes factors / log-likelihood contributions over ad-hoc scoring.
- Guarantees: where prediction/thresholding exists, prefer distribution-free calibration (conformal prediction) or anytime-valid testing (e-process) so we can stop early without invalid inference.
- Graceful degradation: define capability tiers + deterministic fallbacks; never fail closed without a traceable reason.


## Extreme Optimization
- Baseline first: capture p50/p95/p99 for relevant command(s) with `hyperfine` (store raw output).
- Profile next: CPU flamegraph + allocation profile; identify top-5 hotspots.
- Opportunity matrix: implement only Score ≥ 2.0 (Impact×Confidence/Effort).
- Isomorphism proof per change: ordering, tie-breaking, RNG seeds, and golden checksums must match.
- Regression gates: add microbench + budgets; failures must print actionable deltas and include log artifacts.


## Testing Plan
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures.
- Property tests: invariants (idempotence, monotonicity, determinism) where applicable.
- Fuzz tests: parsers/state machines and any input-facing logic.
- Determinism: fixed seeds/time-freeze; snapshot fixtures must be stable.

 {"id":"bd-yzu","title":"Implement Flex layout with constraint solving","description":"PHASE 5 - FLEX LAYOUT

## Background
Flex layout provides 1D constraint-based sizing for rows/columns. Core layout primitive that most complex layouts build upon. Based on CSS Flexbox concepts adapted for terminal grid.

## Technical Requirements
- Flex struct with direction (Row/Column)
- Constraint types:
  - Fixed(u16): exact size
  - Percent(f32): percentage of parent
  - Min(u16): minimum size, grow to fill
  - Max(u16): maximum size, shrink to fit
  - Ratio(u16, u16): proportional sharing
- Constraint solving algorithm:
  1. Allocate Fixed constraints first
  2. Calculate Percent based on remaining space
  3. Distribute remainder to Min/Ratio proportionally
  4. Clamp Max constraints
- Gap/spacing between items
- Cross-axis alignment: Start, Center, End, Stretch

## Design Rationale
Flex is simpler than full 2D grid but handles 90% of TUI layouts. Constraint types mirror Ratatui's proven model. Solving algorithm is deterministic for predictable layouts.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Flex struct with direction and items
- [ ] Full constraint type enum
- [ ] Deterministic constraint solver
- [ ] Gap/margin support
- [ ] Cross-axis alignment
- [ ] Nested Flex support
- [ ] Property tests for constraint invariants
- [ ] Edge case handling (overflow, zero space)

## Performance Budget
- Layout solve for 20 items: < 100µs
- No allocations for typical layouts (stack buffer)

 {"id":"bd-z8q6","title":"Migrate simple buffer-only widgets to Frame (Category A)","description":"## Scope

Migrate all Category A widgets - those that only write to buffer and don't need
any special Frame features.

## Migration Pattern

For each widget:

1. Change signature:
   ```rust
   // FROM:
   fn render(&self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
   
   // TO:
   fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
   ```

2. Replace all `buf` references with `frame.buffer`:
   ```rust
   // FROM:
   buf.get_mut(x, y)
   
   // TO:
   frame.buffer.get_mut(x, y)
   ```

3. Or use a local binding for cleaner code:
   ```rust
   fn render(&self, area: Rect, frame: &mut Frame) {
       let buf = &mut frame.buffer;
       // ... rest of code unchanged
   }
   ```

## Widgets Expected in This Category

Based on functionality (to be confirmed by audit):
- Block (borders, title)
- Rule (horizontal/vertical lines)
- Scrollbar (decorative)
- Spinner (animation frames)
- Paragraph (text display)
- Progress (progress bars)

## Important

- DO NOT change any widget behavior
- DO NOT add new Frame features unless explicitly needed
- This is a MECHANICAL migration only
- If a widget turns out to need Frame features, move it to appropriate category

## Verification

After migration:
- `cargo check -p ftui-widgets` must pass
- All existing tests must pass","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-01T23:21:57.095832290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T23:25:44.437859214Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T23:25:44.437828416Z","close_reason":"All Category A widgets migrated to Frame: Block, Rule, Scrollbar, Spinner, Paragraph, Progress, Panel, Padding, List, Table, StatusLine, ErrorBoundary, CachedWidget, ConstraintOverlay, DebugOverlay. cargo test passes.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-z8q6","depends_on_id":"bd-2ryo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-z8q6","depends_on_id":"bd-3coz","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
